example.vi: Tôi có ít thời gian để hoàn thành bài tập.
example.en: I have little time to finish the homework.
example.es: Tengo poco tiempo para terminar la tarea.

notes_md:
**ít** = few / a little (small amount or small number). It means not much.

Use **ít + noun** for both countable and uncountable nouns (ít tiền, ít người). It usually comes before the noun and doesn’t change form.  
You can add degree words: **rất ít** (very few), **hơi ít** (a bit few).  
For comparison: **ít hơn** = fewer / less than.  

Watch out: **ít** is the opposite of **nhiều** (many / much). Also, **ít khi** means “rarely” — different meaning but very common.
exampleSentences: Em chỉ có ít thời gian để làm bài tập hôm nay.
Anh mua ít rau cho bữa tối vì nhà còn nhiều thức ăn.
Chị thấy trong lớp hôm nay có ít học sinh đến hơn mọi khi.

translation.en: I only eat a little cake.

content.sentence: Họ có ___ thông tin về sự kiện.

translation.en: They have little information about the event.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I only have few time to do homework today.
He bought few vegetables for dinner because there is still a lot of food at home.
She noticed that few students came to class today compared to usual.
